name: These Two Hands
battleMessage: Dad uses The Two Hands, the oldest repair technique known to fathers.
battleMessages: Dad uses The Two Hands, the oldest repair technique known to fathers.
Dad pounds on it like he's adjusting the reception on a 1980's TV.
Flow like a butterfly, sting like a bee.
Dad kick so much ass, my feet stink.
Come on over here, I'm about to fold you up like a 4th grade love letter.
You campaigning for an ass whoopin' son, cuz you about to get elected.

name: Percussive Maintenance
battleMessage: Dad pounds on it like he's adjusting the reception on a 1980's TV.
battleMessages: Dad pounds on it like he's adjusting the reception on a 1980's TV.
Dad gives it the old family troubleshooting method: hit it and look confident.
Dad delivers a corrective thump with warranty-voiding enthusiasm.
Dad applies percussive maintenance. Somewhere, an appliance manual weeps.
Dad knocks on it like the problem might answer the door.
Dad slaps it once, pauses, then slaps it again for science.
description: The oldest repair technique known to fathers.

name: Ibuprofen
description: Restores enough resolve to continue dealing with the house.

name: Adjustable Wrench
battleMessage: Dad applies the Adjustable Wrench with hard-earned driveway confidence.
battleMessages: Dad applies the Adjustable Wrench with hard-earned driveway confidence.
Dad tightens nothing in particular, but the threat respects the gesture.
Dad swings the wrench like a man who has watched three repair videos back-to-back.
Dad gives the wrench a quarter turn and the situation regrets becoming mechanical.
Dad raises the wrench. Several nearby bolts reconsider their life choices.
Dad performs a precision adjustment with extremely non-precision energy.
description: A practical tool and, in desperate times, a statement.

name: Plumbing Snake
battleMessage: Dad feeds the Plumbing Snake into the unknown with grim determination.
battleMessages: Dad feeds the Plumbing Snake into the unknown with grim determination.
Dad cranks the handle like he's starting a lawnmower that owes him money.
Dad commits to a depth he will regret describing to his wife later.
The snake goes in. Dad's dignity does not come back out the same.
Dad mutters 'just a little further' to a 25-foot length of flexible steel.
Dad performs the ancient ritual: insert, twist, pray.
description: Cheap, effective, and deeply unpleasant. Home Depot aisle 14.

name: Plunger of the Gods
battleMessage: Dad raises the Plunger of the Gods. Lesser clogs flee on sight.
battleMessages: Dad raises the Plunger of the Gods. Lesser clogs flee on sight.
Behold: the Plunger of the Gods, $44.99 plus tax, worth every cent.
Dad achieves perfect suction. A single tear of pride is shed.
The Plunger of the Gods does not negotiate. It only plunges.
Dad swings it like Excalibur, if Excalibur was rated for 6-inch drains.
With great suction comes great responsibility.
description: THEE Home Depot's finest. Expensive. Mythic. Worth the drive.
